prerequisites: A running WooCommerce store on WordPress (WooCommerce 3.5 or later, WordPress 4.4 or later).
Admin access to WP-Admin to generate REST API keys under WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API.
Pretty permalinks enabled (WordPress Settings > Permalinks set to any option other than 'Plain'), which the WooCommerce REST API requires.
The store base URL or domain to set as the 'shop' config field.
A 'start_date' to bound the initial/incremental sync window.
HTTPS is recommended so credentials can be sent via HTTP Basic Auth; non-HTTPS endpoints require OAuth 1.0a signing instead.

authMethods.name: REST API key (Consumer Key + Consumer Secret)
authMethods.summary: WooCommerce authenticates with a generated Consumer Key and Consumer Secret. Provide the Consumer Key (prefixed ck_) as the 'api_key' field and the Consumer Secret (prefixed cs_) as the 'api_secret' field. Over HTTPS these are sent as HTTP Basic Auth (key as username, secret as password). Keys are created in WP-Admin under WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API, scoped to a WordPress user and a Read / Write / Read-Write permission level.

setupSteps.title: Enable pretty permalinks
setupSteps.body: In WP-Admin, go to Settings > Permalinks and select any structure other than 'Plain'. The WooCommerce REST API will not route requests correctly with plain permalinks.

setupSteps.title: Create a REST API key in WooCommerce
setupSteps.body: Go to WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API and click Add Key (Create an API key). Enter a Description, choose the WordPress User the key acts as, and set Permissions to Read (this source only reads data). Click Generate API Key.

setupSteps.title: Capture the Consumer Key and Consumer Secret
setupSteps.body: WooCommerce displays a Consumer Key (begins with ck_) and a Consumer Secret (begins with cs_). The secret is shown only once, so copy both immediately. Set the Consumer Key as 'api_key' and the Consumer Secret as 'api_secret' in the connector config.

setupSteps.title: Set the shop and start_date
setupSteps.body: Set 'shop' to your WooCommerce store URL/domain so the connector targets the correct REST API endpoint, and set 'start_date' to the earliest date from which records should be synced.

setupSteps.title: Optionally tune parallelism
setupSteps.body: Leave 'num_workers' unset to use the default, or set it to control how many parallel workers fetch data from the WooCommerce REST API.

sources.title: WooCommerce REST API Documentation (generating keys, permalinks)
